2606.09758.title: Difference-Aware Retrieval Policies for Imitation Learning
2606.09758.paper_summary: Parametric imitation learning via behavior cloning can suffer from poor generalization to out-of-distribution states due to compounding errors during deployment. We show that reusing the training data during inference via a semi-parametric retrieval-based imitation learning approach can alleviate this challenge. We present Difference-Aware Retrieval Policies for Imitation Learning (DARP), a semi-parametric retrieval-based imitation learning approach that addresses this limitation by reparameterizing the imitation learning problem in terms of local neighborhood structure rather than direct state-to-action mappings. Instead of learning a global policy, DARP trains a model to predict actions based on $k$-nearest neighbors from expert demonstrations, their corresponding actions, and the relative distance vectors between neighbor states and query states. DARP requires no additional assumptions beyond those made for standard behavior cloning -- it does not require additional data collection, online expert feedback, or task-specific knowledge. We demonstrate consistent performance improvements of 15-46% over standard behavior cloning across diverse domains, including continuous control and robotic manipulation, and across different representations, including high-dimensional visual features. Code and demos are available at https://weirdlabuw.github.io/darp-site/.
2606.09758.paper_summary_zh: 參數化模仿學習透過行為複製可能會因為在部署過程中累積的錯誤而導致對於分布外狀態的泛化能力差。我們展示了通過一種半參數檢索基礎的模仿學習方法，在推理過程中重用訓練數據可以緩解這一挑戰。我們提出了差異感知檢索策略（Difference-Aware Retrieval Policies for Imitation Learning, DARP），這是一種半參數檢索基礎的模仿學習方法，通過重新參數化模仿學習問題，使其以局部鄰域結構而非直接的狀態到行動映射來解決這一限制。DARP並不學習全局策略，而是訓練模型根據來自專家演示的$k$-最近鄰、其相應的行動以及鄰居狀態與查詢狀態之間的相對距離向量來預測行動。DARP不需要超出標準行為複製所做的額外假設——它不需要額外的數據收集、在線專家反饋或任務特定知識。我們展示了在各種領域（包括連續控制和機器人操作）以及不同表示（包括高維視覺特徵）中，DARP相對於標準行為複製的一致性能提升為15-46%。代碼和演示可在 https://weirdlabuw.github.io/darp-site/ 獲得。
2606.09758.author: Quinn Pfeifer et.al.
2606.09758.authors: Quinn Pfeifer, Ethan Pronovost, Paarth Shah, Khimya Khetarpal, Siddhartha Srinivasa, Abhishek Gupta
